description_long: This is a large, four-engine propeller airplane with a robust, metallic fuselage and a classic mid-20th-century design. The wings are straight and mounted at a mid-level on the fuselage, each supporting two engines with prominent propellers. The tail section features a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers extending from either side. The cockpit is positioned at the front of the fuselage and appears to be enclosed, with a slightly raised canopy. The landing gear is not clearly visible, but the overall structure suggests a heavy, multi-passenger or cargo aircraft. The surface finish is glossy, reflecting light and giving the airplane a polished, metallic appearance.
description: A large, four-engine propeller airplane with a metallic, glossy finish and a conventional tail. The wings are straight and mid-mounted, and the cockpit is enclosed at the front.
description_view_0: Front view showing the four propellers, wide wingspan, and the central cockpit canopy.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the length of the fuselage, engine placement, and the tail structure.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, horizontal stabilizers, and the alignment of the engines along the wings.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view, similar to view 1, showing the other side of the fuselage and wing configuration.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Four piston engines, two mounted on each wing.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Not clearly visible, but likely retractable with multiple main gear units and a nose gear.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a multi-seat configuration and a raised canopy.
